title: Student — Assignments & Grades
content:
Overview


The Assignments and Grades pages show students what their teachers have assigned and how they are performing. Assignments displays tasks sent by teachers. My Grades shows grades and teacher comments organized by term and year.



Assignments Page


The Assignments page lists all assignments that teachers have sent to the student. Each assignment card shows:


  • Title: The assignment name (e.g., "Unit 3 Practice" or "Chapter 5 Review")
  • Subject: Which subject the assignment is for
  • Teacher: The teacher who sent it
  • Description: Detailed instructions from the teacher
  • Date sent: When the assignment was issued

Assignments are read-only — students view them as task lists guiding what to study or practice. They cannot submit work through the portal. The assignment description tells students exactly what to do (e.g., "Complete Unit 3 Quizzes 1 to 5" or "Study the past questions because your test will be similar").


If no assignments have been sent yet, the page shows an empty state: "Loading assignments..." until data is fetched, or a message indicating no assignments are available.



My Grades Page


The My Grades page displays the student's grades across all enrolled subjects. Before grades are shown, the student selects:


  • Term: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Winter Semester, September Term, January Term, or May Term
  • Year: Using plus/minus buttons to change the academic year

Once a term and year are selected, a table displays:


  • Subject: Each enrolled subject for that term
  • Grades: Scores for classwork, midterms, finals, and assignments as entered by the teacher
  • Teacher comments: A comment icon appears next to subjects where the teacher has left feedback

Clicking the comment icon opens a modal showing the full teacher comment. The modal displays the subject, teacher name, and the comment text.



How Grades Are Entered


Grades are entered by teachers through the Gradebook in the Teacher Portal. Students cannot edit or change their grades. If a student believes a grade is incorrect, they should contact their teacher directly. School admins can view grades through the Admin Portal but cannot modify them.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Assignments are study guides from your teachers — they tell you what to work on, not something to submit."
  • "Check the description for detailed instructions from your teacher."
  • "Your grades are organized by term and year. Make sure you've selected the right ones to see the correct grades."
  • "If you see a comment icon next to a grade, click it to read your teacher's feedback."
  • "If you think a grade is wrong, contact your teacher. Support agents and admins cannot change grades."
